猜想.摘要: Best-available closure keeps 'ICT' ahead of 'TICT', but repeated verifier-local limitations prevented further external shrinkage of the unresolved gap: Closure-quality exact-molecule discrimination between ICT and TICT is still missing because verifier evidence is only close-family and the repeated microscopic baseline route is stalled before producing discriminative internal observables.

最终证据摘要: Top1 is ICT (0.58) and top2 is TICT (0.30). I interpret the latest verifier evidence as mixed but still net-supportive of ICT: it reinforces close-family donor-acceptor, solvatochromic, AIE/RIR, cationic CT interpretations, while also explicitly preserving credible TICT-related themes such as twisted geometry, dual-emission/viscosity dependence, and oscillator-strength concerns. This verifier pass does not reduce any remaining portfolio coverage debt because portfolio screening was already complete and no new named alternative appeared. A still-credible alternative does remain, but it is TICT as an already directly screened pairwise challenger rather than an untested hypothesis. The case therefore legitimately stays in pairwise contraction rather than returning to portfolio screening. Closure justification is still collecting, not sufficient and not blocked in principle, because the verifier evidence is only close-family and no exact-molecule discriminative internal observable has been obtained. With 11 rounds remaining, the case is not ready for decisive finalize or best-available finalize yet; it needs one bounded, more discriminative follow-up rather than another generic repetition.

Best-available closure keeps 'ICT' ahead of 'TICT', but repeated verifier-local limitations prevented further external shrinkage of the unresolved gap: Closure-quality exact-molecule discrimination between ICT and TICT is still missing because verifier evidence is only close-family and the repeated microscopic baseline route is stalled before producing discriminative internal observables.

Verifier supplementation for ICT versus TICT remains sufficient at close-family specificity. The latest pass is mixed: it supports ICT-like donor-acceptor CT/AIE interpretations while preserving credible TICT-style diagnostics, and it confirms that no exact-molecule literature hit was found.

Closure justification is still collecting because the present preference for ICT over TICT still relies on structural proxies plus close-family external precedent rather than an exact-molecule discriminative result.

Portfolio screening remains complete after verifier review. The verifier evidence does not create new portfolio debt or introduce a new named mechanism; it only sharpens the already-active ICT versus TICT pair.

Macro capability has already done its bounded job and should not be reused for this pairwise gap. The microscopic run_baseline_bundle route has shown repeated failure on the same observable route and should be treated as stalled unless the observable or route changes. Verifier capability is functioning and still potentially useful, but only if narrowly constrained to pairwise-discriminative evidence rather than another generic close-family summary. Recent working memory repeats the same verifier local uncertainty, so repeating the same verifier route is unlikely to shrink the current gap: Verifier evidence cards summarize external supervision but do not replace Planner-level synthesis; unresolved local gap: the evidence cards still need Planner-level synthesis before any mechanism decision.

Latest verifier retrieval returned 4 close-family evidence cards spanning AIE, ICT, RIR, donor-acceptor behavior, multi-rotor motifs, solvatochromism, TICT-related themes, dual emission, viscosity dependence, twisted geometry, oscillator-strength considerations, and explicit retrieval limitation with no exact-identity literature hit. Internal exact-molecule microscopic evidence remains unavailable because the baseline_bundle route failed again before generating state-ordering or charge-localization observables.
